member.name: F:Wolverine.RabbitMQ.DeadLetterQueueMode.Native
member.summary: Opt into using Rabbit MQ's native dead letter queue approach. This is the default and recommended

member.name: F:Wolverine.RabbitMQ.DeadLetterQueueMode.InteropFriendly
member.summary: When interoperating with some other messaging tools that do not support Rabbit MQ's dead letter queueing functionality or do so differently than Wolverine, it may be necessary to use this option to enable Wolverine to move messages to the dead letter queue in a programmatic way that will not interfere with other messaging tools

member.name: F:Wolverine.RabbitMQ.DeadLetterQueueMode.WolverineStorage
member.summary: Completely ignore Rabbit MQ native dead letter queueing in favor of Wolverine persistent dead letter queueing

member.name: M:Wolverine.RabbitMQ.DynamicObjectCreationExtensions.ModifyRabbitMqObjects(Wolverine.Runtime.IWolverineRuntime,System.Action{Wolverine.RabbitMQ.RabbitMqObjects})
member.summary: Runtime creation of Rabbit MQ queues, exchanges, and bindings.

member.name: M:Wolverine.RabbitMQ.DynamicObjectCreationExtensions.UnBindRabbitMqQueue(Wolverine.Runtime.IWolverineRuntime,System.String,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Will un-bind a queue to an exchange

member.name: M:Wolverine.RabbitMQ.RabbitMqObjects.DeclareExchange(System.String)
member.summary: Declare a new exchange. Will not be created if it already exists on the Rabbit MQ broker. Will throw an exception if the declared configuration is different from the existing exchange

member.name: M:Wolverine.RabbitMQ.RabbitMqObjects.DeclareQueue(System.String)
member.summary: Declare a new queue. Will not be created it it already exists on the Rabbit MQ broker. Will throw an exception if the declared configuration is different from the existing queue

member.name: P:Wolverine.RabbitMQ.Internal.RabbitMqEndpoint.EnvelopeMapper
member.summary: When set, overrides the built in envelope mapping with a custom implementation

member.name: M:Wolverine.RabbitMQ.Internal.RabbitMqEnvelope.OverrideDeliveryTag(System.UInt64)
member.summary: This is only here for chaos testing. Don't use this for any other reason. You've been warned!

member.name: T:Wolverine.RabbitMQ.Internal.IRabbitMqEnvelopeMapper
member.summary: Responsible for mapping incoming and outgoing Wolverine Envelope objects to the Rabbit MQ IBasicProperties object. Custom implementations of this can be used to create interoperability with non-Wolverine applications through Rabbit MQ

member.name: M:Wolverine.RabbitMQ.Internal.RabbitMqExchange.BindTopic(System.String)
member.summary: Declare a Rabbit MQ binding with the supplied topic pattern to the queue

member.name: M:Wolverine.RabbitMQ.Internal.TopicBinding.ToQueue(System.String,System.Action{Wolverine.RabbitMQ.IRabbitMqQueue})
member.summary: Create a binding of the topic pattern previously specified to a Rabbit Mq queue
member.param: The name of the Rabbit Mq queue

member.name: P:Wolverine.RabbitMQ.Internal.RabbitMqQueue.PreFetchCount
member.summary: The number of unacknowledged messages that can be processed concurrently

member.name: P:Wolverine.RabbitMQ.Internal.RabbitMqQueue.DeadLetterQueue
member.summary: Use to override the dead letter queue for this queue

member.name: P:Wolverine.RabbitMQ.Internal.RabbitMqQueue.AutoDelete
member.summary: If true, this queue will be deleted when the connection is closed. This is mostly useful for temporary, response queues

member.name: P:Wolverine.RabbitMQ.Internal.RabbitMqQueue.IsExclusive
member.summary: If true, this queue can only be used by a single connection

member.name: P:Wolverine.RabbitMQ.Internal.RabbitMqQueue.IsDurable
member.summary: The default is true. Governs whether queue messages

member.name: P:Wolverine.RabbitMQ.Internal.RabbitMqQueue.Arguments
member.summary: Arguments for Rabbit MQ queue declarations. See the Rabbit MQ .NET client documentation at https://www.rabbitmq.com/dotnet.html

member.name: M:Wolverine.RabbitMQ.Internal.RabbitMqQueue.TimeToLive(System.TimeSpan)
member.summary: Create a "time to live" limit for messages in this queue. Sets the Rabbit MQ x-message-ttl argument on a queue

member.name: P:Wolverine.RabbitMQ.Internal.RabbitMqQueue.PurgeOnStartup
member.summary: Declare that Wolverine should purge the existing queue of all existing messages on startup

member.name: M:Wolverine.RabbitMQ.Internal.RabbitMqQueue.QueuedCount
member.summary: Mostly for testing

member.name: M:Wolverine.RabbitMQ.Internal.RabbitMqTransportExpression.ConfigureConnection(System.Action{RabbitMQ.Client.ConnectionFactory})
member.summary: Make any necessary customizations to the Rabbit MQ client's ConnectionFactory

member.name: M:Wolverine.RabbitMQ.Internal.RabbitMqTransportExpression.UseConventionalRouting(System.Action{Wolverine.RabbitMQ.RabbitMqMessageRoutingConvention})
member.summary: Opt into using conventional Rabbit MQ routing based on the message types

member.name: M:Wolverine.RabbitMQ.Internal.RabbitMqTransportExpression.DeclareExchange(System.String,System.Action{Wolverine.RabbitMQ.IRabbitMqExchange})
member.summary: Declare a new exchange without impacting message routing or listening. The default exchange type is "fan out". This does not respect identifier prefixes!

member.name: M:Wolverine.RabbitMQ.Internal.RabbitMqTransportExpression.BindExchange(System.String,Wolverine.RabbitMQ.ExchangeType)
member.summary: Declare a binding from a Rabbit Mq exchange to a Rabbit MQ queue. This does not respect identifier prefixes!

member.name: M:Wolverine.RabbitMQ.Internal.RabbitMqTransportExpression.DeclareQueue(System.String,System.Action{Wolverine.RabbitMQ.IRabbitMqQueue})
member.summary: Declare that a queue should be created with the supplied name and optional configuration. . This does not respect identifier prefixes!

member.name: M:Wolverine.RabbitMQ.Internal.RabbitMqTransportExpression.DisableDeadLetterQueueing
member.summary: Disable Wolverine's automatic dead letter queue setup, so it will not override dead letter queue exchange usage per queue or try to create Rabbit Mq objects for dead letter queueing

member.name: M:Wolverine.RabbitMQ.Internal.RabbitMqTransportExpression.DisableSystemRequestReplyQueueDeclaration
member.summary: Disable Wolverine's automatic Request/Reply queue declaration for a specific node

member.name: M:Wolverine.RabbitMQ.Internal.RabbitMqTransportExpression.UseListenerConnectionOnly
member.summary: Turn on listener connection only in case if you only need to listen for messages The sender connection won't be activated in this case

member.name: M:Wolverine.RabbitMQ.Internal.RabbitMqTransportExpression.UseSenderConnectionOnly
member.summary: Turn on sender connection only in case if you only need to send messages The listener connection won't be created in this case

member.name: M:Wolverine.RabbitMQ.Internal.RabbitMqTransportExpression.EnableWolverineControlQueues
member.summary: Direct Rabbit MQ queues as the control queues between Wolverine nodes This is more efficient than the built in Wolverine database control queues if Rabbit MQ is an option

member.name: M:Wolverine.RabbitMQ.Internal.RabbitMqTransportExpression.BindingExpression.ToQueue(System.String,System.Action{Wolverine.RabbitMQ.IRabbitMqQueue})
member.summary: Bind the named exchange to a queue. The routing key will be [exchange name]_[queue name]
member.param: Optional configuration of the Rabbit MQ queue

member.name: M:Wolverine.RabbitMQ.Internal.RabbitMqTransportExpression.BindingExpression.ToQueue(System.String,System.String,System.Action{Wolverine.RabbitMQ.IRabbitMqQueue},System.Collections.Generic.Dictionary{System.String,System.Object})
member.summary: Bind the named exchange to a queue with a user supplied binding key
member.param: Optional configuration of the Rabbit MQ queue
Optional configuration for arguments to the Rabbit MQ binding

member.name: M:Wolverine.RabbitMQ.Internal.RabbitMqTransportExpression.CustomizeDeadLetterQueueing(Wolverine.RabbitMQ.DeadLetterQueue)
member.summary: Customize the dead letter queueing to override the exchange or queue setup

member.name: P:Wolverine.RabbitMQ.IRabbitMqExchange.IsDurable
member.summary: Should this exchange survive server restarts and last until they are explicitly deleted. The default is true

member.name: P:Wolverine.RabbitMQ.IRabbitMqExchange.ExchangeType
member.summary: Type of Rabbit MQ exchange. The default is fanout

member.name: P:Wolverine.RabbitMQ.IRabbitMqExchange.AutoDelete
member.summary: If true, this exchange will be deleted when the connection is closed. Default is false

member.name: M:Wolverine.RabbitMQ.IRabbitMqExchange.BindQueue(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Bind a named queue to this exchange with an optional binding key

member.name: M:Wolverine.RabbitMQ.IRabbitMqExchange.BindTopic(System.String)
member.summary: Declare a Rabbit MQ binding with the supplied topic pattern to the queue

member.name: P:Wolverine.RabbitMQ.IRabbitMqQueue.AutoDelete
member.summary: If true, this queue will be deleted when the connection is closed. This is mostly useful for temporary, response queues

member.name: P:Wolverine.RabbitMQ.IRabbitMqQueue.IsExclusive
member.summary: If true, this queue can only be used by a single connection

member.name: P:Wolverine.RabbitMQ.IRabbitMqQueue.IsDurable
member.summary: The default is true. Governs whether queue messages

member.name: P:Wolverine.RabbitMQ.IRabbitMqQueue.Arguments
member.summary: Arguments for Rabbit MQ queue declarations. See the Rabbit MQ .NET client documentation at https://www.rabbitmq.com/dotnet.html

member.name: P:Wolverine.RabbitMQ.IRabbitMqQueue.PurgeOnStartup
member.summary: Declare that Wolverine should purge the existing queue of all existing messages on startup

member.name: M:Wolverine.RabbitMQ.IRabbitMqQueue.TimeToLive(System.TimeSpan)
member.summary: Create a "time to live" limit for messages in this queue. Sets the Rabbit MQ x-message-ttl argument on a queue

member.name: M:Wolverine.RabbitMQ.RabbitMqListenerConfiguration.CircuitBreaker(System.Action{Wolverine.ErrorHandling.CircuitBreakerOptions})
member.summary: Add circuit breaker exception handling to this listener

member.name: M:Wolverine.RabbitMQ.RabbitMqListenerConfiguration.PreFetchCount(System.UInt16)
member.summary: Override the Rabbit MQ PreFetchCount value for just this endpoint for how many messages can be pre-fetched into memory before being handled

member.name: M:Wolverine.RabbitMQ.RabbitMqListenerConfiguration.UseInterop(Wolverine.RabbitMQ.Internal.IRabbitMqEnvelopeMapper)
member.summary: Use a custom interoperability strategy to map Wolverine messages to an upstream system's protocol

member.name: M:Wolverine.RabbitMQ.RabbitMqListenerConfiguration.UseMassTransitInterop(System.Action{Wolverine.Runtime.Interop.MassTransit.IMassTransitInterop})
member.summary: Add MassTransit interoperability to this Rabbit MQ listening endpoint
member.param: Optionally configure the JSON serialization on this endpoint

member.name: M:Wolverine.RabbitMQ.RabbitMqListenerConfiguration.UseNServiceBusInterop
member.summary: Add NServiceBus interoperability to this Rabbit MQ listening endpoint

member.name: M:Wolverine.RabbitMQ.RabbitMqListenerConfiguration.TimeToLive(System.TimeSpan)
member.summary: Create a "time to live" limit for messages in this queue. Sets the Rabbit MQ x-message-ttl argument on a queue

member.name: M:Wolverine.RabbitMQ.RabbitMqListenerConfiguration.ConfigureQueue(System.Action{Wolverine.RabbitMQ.IRabbitMqQueue})
member.summary: Make any customizations to the underlying Rabbit MQ Queue when Wolverine is building the queues

member.name: M:Wolverine.RabbitMQ.RabbitMqListenerConfiguration.DeadLetterQueueing(Wolverine.RabbitMQ.DeadLetterQueue)
member.summary: Customize the dead letter queueing for this specific endpoint

member.name: M:Wolverine.RabbitMQ.RabbitMqListenerConfiguration.DisableDeadLetterQueueing
member.summary: Remove all dead letter queueing declarations from this queue

member.name: T:Wolverine.RabbitMQ.RabbitMqMessageRoutingConvention
member.summary: Conventional message routing for Rabbit MQ. By default, sends messages to an exchange named after the MessageTypeName that is bound to a queue of the same name.

member.name: M:Wolverine.RabbitMQ.RabbitMqMessageRoutingConvention.ExchangeNameForSending(System.Func{System.Type,System.String})
member.summary: Conventional name for the exchange .Same as IdentifierForSender

member.name: M:Wolverine.RabbitMQ.RabbitMqSubscriberConfiguration.UseMassTransitInterop(System.Action{Wolverine.Runtime.Interop.MassTransit.IMassTransitInterop})
member.summary: Configure this Rabbit MQ endpoint for interoperability with MassTransit
member.param: Optionally configure the JSON serialization for MassTransit

member.name: M:Wolverine.RabbitMQ.RabbitMqSubscriberConfiguration.UseNServiceBusInterop
member.summary: Configure this Rabbit MQ endpoint for interoperability with NServiceBus

member.name: M:Wolverine.RabbitMQ.RabbitMqSubscriberConfiguration.UseInterop(Wolverine.RabbitMQ.Internal.IRabbitMqEnvelopeMapper)
member.summary: Use a custom interoperability strategy to map Wolverine messages to an upstream system's protocol

member.name: M:Wolverine.RabbitMQ.RabbitMqExchangeConfiguration.UseMassTransitInterop(System.Action{Wolverine.Runtime.Interop.MassTransit.IMassTransitInterop})
member.summary: Configure this Rabbit MQ endpoint for interoperability with MassTransit
member.param: Optionally configure the JSON serialization for MassTransit

member.name: M:Wolverine.RabbitMQ.RabbitMqExchangeConfiguration.UseNServiceBusInterop
member.summary: Configure this Rabbit MQ endpoint for interoperability with NServiceBus

member.name: M:Wolverine.RabbitMQ.RabbitMqExchangeConfiguration.ExchangeType(Wolverine.RabbitMQ.ExchangeType)
member.summary: Modify the exchange type, the default is fan out

member.name: M:Wolverine.RabbitMQ.RabbitMqTransportExtensions.RabbitMqTransport(Wolverine.WolverineOptions)
member.summary: Quick access to the Rabbit MQ Transport within this application. This is for advanced usage

member.name: M:Wolverine.RabbitMQ.RabbitMqTransportExtensions.PublishMessagesToRabbitMqExchange``1(Wolverine.WolverineOptions,System.String,System.Func{``0,System.String})
member.summary: Publish messages that are of type T or could be cast to type T to a Rabbit MQ topic exchange using the supplied function to determine the topic for the message

member.name: M:Wolverine.RabbitMQ.RabbitMqTransportExtensions.UseRabbitMq(Wolverine.WolverineOptions,System.Action{RabbitMQ.Client.ConnectionFactory})
member.summary: Configure connection and authentication information about the Rabbit MQ usage within this Wolverine application

member.name: M:Wolverine.RabbitMQ.RabbitMqTransportExtensions.UseRabbitMq(Wolverine.WolverineOptions,System.Uri)
member.summary: Connect to Rabbit MQ on the local machine with all the default Rabbit MQ client options
member.param: Rabbit MQ Uri that designates the connection information. See https://www.rabbitmq.com/uri-spec.html

member.name: M:Wolverine.RabbitMQ.RabbitMqTransportExtensions.UseRabbitMq(Wolverine.WolverineOptions)
member.summary: Connect to Rabbit MQ on the local machine with all the default Rabbit MQ client options

member.name: M:Wolverine.RabbitMQ.RabbitMqTransportExtensions.UseRabbitMq(Wolverine.WolverineOptions,System.String)
member.summary: Connect to Rabbit MQ using the values from the connection string. This format is purposely designed to be compatible with the syntax from https://docs.particular.net/transports/rabbitmq/connection-settings

member.name: M:Wolverine.RabbitMQ.RabbitMqTransportExtensions.ToRabbitTopic(Wolverine.Configuration.IPublishToExpression,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Publish matching messages to Rabbit MQ using the named routing key or queue name and optionally an exchange
member.param: A static topic name if the exchange is topic-based

member.name: M:Wolverine.RabbitMQ.RabbitMqTransportExtensions.ToRabbitQueue(Wolverine.Configuration.IPublishToExpression,System.String,System.Action{Wolverine.RabbitMQ.IRabbitMqQueue})
member.summary: Publish matching messages straight to a Rabbit MQ queue using the named routing key or queue name and optionally an exchange
member.param: This is used as the routing key when publishing. Can be either a binding key or a queue name or a static topic name if the exchange is topic-based

member.name: M:Wolverine.RabbitMQ.RabbitMqTransportExtensions.ToRabbitExchange(Wolverine.Configuration.IPublishToExpression,System.String,System.Action{Wolverine.RabbitMQ.IRabbitMqExchange})
member.summary: Publish matching messages to Rabbit MQ to the designated exchange. This is appropriate for "fanout" exchanges where Rabbit MQ ignores the routing key
member.param: Optional configuration of this exchange if Wolverine is doing the initialization in Rabbit MQ

member.name: M:Wolverine.RabbitMQ.RabbitMqTransportExtensions.ToRabbitRoutingKey(Wolverine.Configuration.IPublishToExpression,System.String,System.String,System.Action{Wolverine.RabbitMQ.IRabbitMqExchange})
member.summary: Publish matching messages to Rabbit MQ to the designated exchange. This is appropriate for "direct" exchanges where Rabbit MQ needs the routing key

member.name: M:Wolverine.RabbitMQ.RabbitMqTransportExtensions.ToRabbitTopics(Wolverine.Configuration.IPublishToExpression,System.String,System.Action{Wolverine.RabbitMQ.IRabbitMqExchange})
member.summary: Publish matching messages to Rabbit MQ to the designated topic exchange, with the topic name derived from the message type name or an explicit override
member.param: Optional configuration of this exchange if Wolverine is doing the initialization in Rabbit MQ
